bead.title: agent: close parent after post-attempt decomposition
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The post-attempt recovery/decomposer path applies the same park-open decomposition pattern as pre-claim intake. A successful lossless post-attempt split should close the decomposed parent while leaving generated children work-ready; it should not strand an open execution-ineligible parent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-133 documents that a successful decomposition creates child beads, sets `execution-eligible=false`, and emits `decompose-applied`.
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:207-210 updates the parent extra metadata to `execution-eligible=false` after child creation instead of closing the parent through the lifecycle/store API.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go currently covers post-attempt too-large no_changes decomposition with old open/ineligible expectations.
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose_test.go:20-22 says the post-ladder decomposer creates child beads and sets parent `execution-eligible=false`, locking in the old lifecycle.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update `runDecomposer` in cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go so successful lossless decomposition closes the parent through the lifecycle/store API after child beads are created.
- Preserve durable `decompose-applied` evidence with generated child IDs and cost/route fields already recorded by the path.
- Clear parent claim metadata and heartbeat fields on the closed parent.
- Ensure post-attempt generated children use `Parent=<decomposed-id>` as hierarchy metadata only and are not blocked by a dependency on the decomposed parent.
- Update post-attempt and post-ladder tests to assert close semantics, child readiness, and `decompose-applied` child ID evidence.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change pre-claim intake decomposition or auto-epic decomposition in this bead; those are separate children under ddx-e9542e0d.
- Do not implement ddx-130cf9ab's broader doctor/back-edge repair work.
- Do not redesign historical `dead_intermediate_close` behavior for already-open containers.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e9542e0d.
Deps: No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. `runDecomposer` in `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go` closes the decomposed parent through the lifecycle/store API after generated child beads are created.
2. `decompose-applied` evidence still includes generated child IDs and existing cost/route metadata.
3. Parent claim metadata and heartbeat fields are cleared on the closed decomposed parent.
4. `TestPostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges_ClosesParentAndLeavesChildrenReady` proves post-attempt orchestrator decomposition closes the parent and generated children remain ready through the `Parent` field when they have no explicit deps.
5. `TestPostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose_ReviewTooLarge_ClosesParent` proves the post-ladder decomposer path closes the parent and records `decompose-applied` with child IDs.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'PostAttemptTooLargeNoChanges|PostLadderExhaustion_TriggersDecompose|DecomposeDoesNotSelfDep|parent_ancestor_in_deps' -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
